features.summary: Deere reported Q2 net sales of $13.37 billion (up 11%) with equipment operations margin of 19.9%. Production and precision ag sales rose 13% to $5.117 billion with 21% operating margin, while small ag and turf sales increased 5% to $3.57 billion with 14.6% margin. The company raised full-year net income guidance to $7.0-7.4 billion and expects equipment operations cash flow of $5.6-6.0 billion. Supply chain constraints continue creating production inefficiencies and elevated inventory of partially completed machines awaiting parts.
features.management_outlook: Management expects strong second-half performance with Q4 being the highest revenue quarter, driven by completing partially built inventory and minimal factory shutdowns. Production and precision ag net sales are forecast up 25-30% for fiscal 2022 with 21-22% operating margins. Order books remain largely full through fiscal 2022 with strong early demand for model year 2023 products. The company anticipates 2023 will be another strong demand year given favorable agricultural fundamentals and continued supply constraints limiting industry ability to meet demand.
features.risks: Persistent supply chain disruptions across multiple components including semiconductors, castings, and hydraulics continue constraining production and driving inefficiencies. Material and freight cost inflation remains elevated with increased use of premium air freight solutions. Geopolitical events in Russia/Ukraine resulted in $93 million in impairments across segments. Rising interest rates may impact construction markets, while fertilizer cost inflation and availability concerns pressure agricultural customers despite strong commodity prices.
features.key_drivers: Strong agricultural fundamentals support demand with global grain/oilseed stocks declining for three consecutive seasons and elevated commodity prices offsetting higher input costs. Fleet aging from two years of constrained production creates pent-up replacement demand. Technology adoption accelerating as customers seek precision solutions to reduce input usage amid cost inflation. Construction markets remain strong driven by elevated housing, oil/gas activity, and rental company refleeting, though compact equipment inventory levels remain extremely low.
